item.title: Gibson, FNM, ZNS, gas prices
item.ns0:title: Gibson, FNM, ZNS, gas prices
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, a Grand Bahama representative brings "shameful" hospital conditions and power concerns to the floor of the House while the opposition formally calls for a police probe into a controversial U.S. affidavit. We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, a Grand Bahama representative brings "shameful" hospital conditions and power concerns to the floor of the House while the opposition formally calls for a police probe into a controversial U.S. affidavit. We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, a Grand Bahama representative brings "shameful" hospital conditions and power concerns to the floor of the House while the opposition formally calls for a police probe into a controversial U.S. affidavit. We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead.
-----

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
